description: OpenTradex v1.0 — Gateway hardening, mobile completion, desktop security, and frictionless onboarding. Rock-solid gateway, every mobile button works end-to-end, code-signed desktop with OS keychain, and onboarding in under 90 seconds.

userStories.title: Gateway: graceful error handling on all routes
userStories.description: As a client developer, I want the gateway to return a consistent JSON error on any failure so my clients don't hang or get HTML error pages.
acceptanceCriteria: Every route in src/gateway/index.ts wrapped so thrown errors return { error: string, code: string } with correct HTTP status
No route can crash the process — uncaught rejections in handlers are logged and responded to
Timeout guard: any route taking > 30s returns 504 with { error: 'Gateway timeout', code: 'TIMEOUT' }
Add tests/gateway.test.ts covering: 404, 401, 400 (bad body), 500 (handler throw), 504 (slow handler)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Gateway error shape standardized to { error, code }. Added readJsonBody helper, per-request timeout guard (configurable via GatewayConfig.timeoutMs, default 30s), SyntaxError → 400 BAD_JSON, unmatched /api/* → 404 NOT_FOUND (no SPA fallthrough). json() + error() guard on res.headersSent. Added src/gateway/gateway.test.ts (6 tests: 404/401/400/500/504/health). Test script now uses tsx. All green.

userStories.title: Gateway: npm test actually runs and passes
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need the test command to exercise real behaviour, not a no-op.
acceptanceCriteria: package.json test script runs all *.test.ts under src/ via node --test with tsx loader
Starter tests cover: config load/save, risk checkTrade, gateway /api/health returns 200, mode-lock enforcement
Add .github/workflows/ci.yml running npm run build && npm test on push
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Test script now runs tsx --test against gateway/config/risk test files. Added src/config.test.ts (8 tests: load/save round-trip, updatedAt advance, mode-lock paper-only/paper-default/live-allowed enforcement, auth token round-trip, hashToken determinism) using HOME override + tmpdir so no user files touched. Added src/risk.test.ts (6 tests: checkRisk allows/blocks/warns, max-open-positions, panicFlatten, getRiskState shape). Added .github/workflows/ci.yml (matrix node 18/20/22 → install/typecheck/build/test). 20/20 tests pass, build exit 0.

userStories.title: Gateway: auth unbypassable in non-local bind modes
userStories.description: As a remote user, I need confidence that no route leaks data when auth is required.
acceptanceCriteria: Audit every path in createGateway — no route skips checkAuth when requireAuth is true, except /api/health and static assets
WebSocket upgrade rejects missing/invalid token with 401 before allocating client state
Constant-time token comparison in verifyAuthToken (no early-return timing leak)
Test: GET /api/scan without token → 401; with wrong token → 401; with valid token → 200
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: verifyAuthToken now uses timingSafeEqual (constant-time) with length-mismatch dummy compare to avoid early-return timing leak. createGateway honours explicit config.requireAuth (falls back to bindMode-derived when absent). Audit: every route is behind /api/, /agent/, /ai/, /x402/ prefix OR in protectedExact list — only /api/health + static assets are exempt. WS upgrade rejects missing/invalid token with 401 before client allocation (pre-existing in handler). Added src/gateway/gateway-auth.test.ts (7 tests: 401 no-token, 401 wrong-bearer, 200 valid-bearer, 200 query-token, health-open, WS no-token 401, WS bad-token 401). 27/27 passing, tsc clean.

userStories.title: Gateway: WebSocket auto-reconnect + backpressure
userStories.description: As a dashboard user, I want my live feed to keep working if the gateway restarts or my laptop sleeps.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/dashboard/src/hooks/useHarness.ts reconnects with exponential backoff (1s → 30s cap)
Reconnected client re-subscribes to same event stream and shows 'reconnected' banner for 2s
Gateway drops slow WS clients (send buffer > 1 MB) with close-code and log line
Heartbeat: clients missing 2 consecutive pings get evicted (verify 30s interval actually evicts)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill (kill gateway, see reconnect)
userStories.notes: Client (packages/dashboard/src/hooks/useHarness.ts): exponential backoff 1s→30s cap (2^attempt), attempt counter resets on open, reconnectedAt timestamp (auto-clears after 2s for banner), latency via client-side JSON ping→pong, SSE fallback re-subscribes on WS drop. WsMeta type added (types.ts) with attempts/latencyMs/reconnectedAt; hook exports wsMeta + connectionType. Gateway: WebSocketClient.missedPings counter, evict after WS_MAX_MISSED_PINGS=2 consecutive miss with log line; backpressure drop when socket.writableLength > 1MB (close code 1013 Try Again Later) with log line. Browser verification still needed once dashboard build runs. Typecheck clean (root + dashboard), 27/27 tests pass.

userStories.title: Desktop: Electron security hardening
userStories.description: As a user installing the desktop app, I want it to follow modern Electron security defaults.
acceptanceCriteria: BrowserWindow uses contextIsolation: true, nodeIntegration: false, sandbox: true
Only preload.ts exposes a narrow window.opentradex surface (no raw ipcRenderer)
CSP header set on loaded HTML: default-src 'self'; connect-src 'self' http://localhost:3210 ws://localhost:3210
Web security enabled; no allowRunningInsecureContent
External links open in system browser via shell.openExternal with allowlist
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: packages/desktop/src/main.ts hardened: webPreferences now sets sandbox:true, webSecurity:true, allowRunningInsecureContent:false (plus existing contextIsolation:true, nodeIntegration:false). CSP_POLICY installed via session.defaultSession.webRequest.onHeadersReceived in app.whenReady() — default-src 'self', connect-src allows http/ws://localhost:3210 + 127.0.0.1:3210 only, frame-ancestors 'none', no unsafe-inline/eval for scripts; also adds X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options:DENY, Referrer-Policy. External links route through openExternalSafe() which checks EXTERNAL_LINK_ALLOWLIST (github.com, discord.gg/com, opentradex.net); non-listed URLs are logged and refused. will-navigate handler cancels any nav away from 127.0.0.1/localhost and forwards allowlisted URLs to the OS browser. shell.openExternal calls in menu (Documentation, Report Issue, Join Discord) and setWindowOpenHandler replaced with openExternalSafe. Preload already used contextBridge; no raw ipcRenderer exposed. npx tsc --noEmit in packages/desktop clean.

userStories.title: Desktop: OS keychain for API keys
userStories.description: As a user, I want my API keys encrypted at rest using my OS keychain, not plain JSON.
acceptanceCriteria: Integrate keytar (or Electron safeStorage) — store anthropic, openai, google, kalshi, polymarket, alpaca credentials there
Migration: on first run of v1.0, if ~/.opentradex/config.json has plain keys, move to keychain and null the file fields
loadConfig() hydrates secrets from keychain at runtime; never logs them
Unit test: mock keychain, verify round-trip write/read
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Added packages/desktop/src/secrets.ts — SecretStore class with injectable SafeCrypto dependency (testable without Electron). File format v2 at ~/.opentradex/ai-keys.json with {enc,v} entries; plaintext fallback 0600 when OS keychain unavailable. main.ts now constructs SecretStore with Electron's safeStorage (Keychain on macOS / DPAPI on Windows / libsecret on Linux), runs migrateToEncrypted() on boot to upgrade any legacy plaintext entries, and injects decryptAll() results as env vars when spawning the gateway (shell-set vars win). Parent-env protection: buildGatewayEnv skips envs already set by the shell. Added IPC handlers secrets:list/save/delete/canEncrypt; preload exposes narrow window.opentradex.secrets surface — renderer never sees plaintext after save. SetupWizard now prefers IPC when available (encrypts via keychain) and shows 'OS keychain' vs '0600 file' copy dynamically; falls back to gateway HTTP endpoint for bare-browser users. packages/desktop/src/secrets.test.ts — 13 unit tests with fake XOR crypto: set/get round-trip, plaintext-never-on-disk, delete, list names-only, reject short values, plaintext fallback, migrateToEncrypted upgrade path, migrate no-op when no crypto, decryptAll, buildGatewayEnv mapping, parent-env respected, null on cross-platform decrypt, corrupt file recovery. 40/40 total tests pass in isolation, all three packages typecheck clean, dashboard vite build succeeds (267KB, 79KB gzip).

userStories.title: Desktop: one-click AI provider setup (detect local CLIs)
userStories.description: As a user, I want the desktop app to auto-detect AI tools I've already installed.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings → AI panel probes for: claude, opencode, gemini, ollama binaries on PATH
Shows detected list with 'Use this' buttons — clicking wires provider in config (no API key needed for CLI providers)
If none detected, shows provider dropdown (Anthropic / OpenAI / Google / OpenRouter / Groq / DeepSeek / Kimi) + API key field + Test button
Test button hits /api/ai/chat with 'say hi', shows success/error inline
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Added src/ai/providers/detect.ts (shared PATH probe — binaryCandidates handles Windows .cmd/.exe/.bat variants) and two new providers: src/ai/providers/gemini-cli.ts (spawns `gemini -p <prompt>`, uses `gemini auth login` creds) and src/ai/providers/ollama.ts (hits OLLAMA_HOST or http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1 OpenAI-compat endpoint, no key needed, detects via `ollama` binary OR OLLAMA_HOST env). Both registered in registry.ts alongside new detectCLIs() helper that returns [{provider, binary, path, available, label, description, requiresApiKey}] for claude / opencode / gemini / ollama. ai-keys.ts v1 store gained optional `preferredProvider` field; hydrateAIKeysFromDisk sets OPENTRADEX_ROLE_ORCHESTRATOR from it so the resolver routes through the user's chosen CLI; setPreferredProvider/getPreferredProvider exposed for the gateway. Gateway endpoints: GET /api/ai/cli-detect returns { detected: [...with .configured and .defaultModel from the registry] }; POST /api/ai/preferred accepts { provider } (string or null), validates it's registered + configured, persists to ai-keys.json, sets env var, re-inits AI singleton, broadcasts 'ai' event. GET /api/ai/providers now also returns `preferred` field. SetupWizard: CLIDetectionEntry type; fetches /api/ai/cli-detect on open (alongside providers list); new 'Detected on your system' section at top of provider step that shows available CLIs as green-accent tiles with 'Use this →' buttons — clicking calls POST /api/ai/preferred and jumps straight to 'done' (no API-key step). Preferred CLI gets an ACTIVE badge. If no CLIs detected, wording switches to 'Pick an AI provider…'. cliLoading shows 'Scanning your system…', cliError surfaces inline. Live smoke against real gateway on port 3214 verified: /api/ai/cli-detect returned claude+opencode available=true (with actual Windows .cmd paths C:\nvm4w\nodejs\claude.cmd and opencode.cmd) and gemini+ollama available=false as expected on this box. POST /api/ai/preferred {provider:'claude-cli'} → {ok:true, preferred:'claude-cli'}; clearing with null also works. All three packages typecheck clean, dashboard vite build succeeds at 270KB/79KB gzip, 40/40 tests pass in isolation (secrets 13, gateway 6, gateway-auth 7, config 8, risk 6).

userStories.title: CLI onboarding: under 5 prompts for paper default
userStories.description: As a new user running `opentradex onboard`, I want to finish in under 90 seconds with sensible defaults.
acceptanceCriteria: --paper-only flag skips all prompts except AI provider (1 prompt) → writes config + starts gateway
Default interactive flow: (1) paper mode Y/n (2) AI provider or skip (3) starting capital [$10k] (4) bind mode [local] (5) done
Each prompt has visible default and accepts Enter to take it
End of flow prints: gateway URL, token (if remote), pair QR (if remote), next-step command
Works non-TTY (CI/scripted): reads env vars OPENTRADEX_MODE, OPENTRADEX_AI_PROVIDER, OPENTRADEX_AI_KEY
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Added runFastOnboard() in src/onboard.ts with 5-prompt flow (mode, AI provider, capital, bind, done summary). --paper-only collapses to 1 optional prompt (AI provider) and auto-selects paper-only + local bind + $10k default capital. Refactored readline to lazy getRL/closeRL so importing the module in tests doesn't hold stdin open; CLI still closes cleanly after summary. Non-TTY path reads OPENTRADEX_MODE/AI_PROVIDER/AI_KEY/CAPITAL/BIND env vars and exits without prompts — verified by 5/5 tests in src/onboard.test.ts (paper-only path, env-var flow with ai-keys.json persistence + no plaintext leak in summary, inputs override win over env, unknown provider skipped gracefully, lan bind auto-generates auth token). Wired runFastOnboard into src/bin/cli.ts: `opentradex onboard [--paper-only]` is now the fast flow; `--full` keeps the legacy 8-step flow. help text updated. LAN IP probed via networkInterfaces() for pair URL hints. Live smoke: HOME=<tmp> OPENTRADEX_MODE=paper-only node dist/bin/cli.js onboard --paper-only → 0 prompts, <1s, exits cleanly, writes config.json+mode.lock+audit/+skills/ under tmp HOME. Tests: src/onboard.test.ts 5/5 pass (296ms total). Typecheck clean at root. Full test suite: 45/47 subtests pass (the 2 'not ok' at file level are pre-existing gateway-test SIGTERM from a 90s wrapper timeout on long-running gateway fixtures — every individual subtest within them reports 'ok').

userStories.title: CLI onboarding: pair QR for mobile
userStories.description: As a user, I want to scan a QR with my phone at the end of onboarding.
acceptanceCriteria: When bindMode is lan or tunnel, onboard prints an ASCII QR encoding {host, token} to terminal
Saves SVG QR to ~/.opentradex/pair.svg for screenshotting
Uses qrcode-terminal for ASCII, qrcode for SVG
Pair QR format documented so mobile app's 'Scan QR' can decode it
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added qrcode + qrcode-terminal + @types/qrcode deps. Created src/pair-qr.ts with stable v1 envelope { v:1, host, token, issuedAt }; exports buildPairEnvelope (validates host+token, stamps version + ms-epoch issuedAt), encodePairEnvelope (JSON.stringify), renderAsciiQR (promise-wrapped qrcode-terminal generate with small:true), writePairSvg (qrcode.toString SVG at 512px/M correction, written to <dir>/pair.svg), and makePairArtifacts (one-shot that returns { envelope, encoded, ascii, svgPath }). qrcode-terminal is CJS-only + ships no types, so bridged it via createRequire(import.meta.url) for ESM safety. runFastOnboard now calls makePairArtifacts whenever a token is issued (bindMode !== 'local'): prints ASCII QR lines via io.print, logs SVG path, and prints the raw JSON payload as a fallback for scanners that can't read the terminal art. FastOnboardResult.summary gained optional pairSvgPath and pairEncoded fields so callers can assert on them. 7/7 tests in src/pair-qr.test.ts pass (envelope shape/version, caller-supplied issuedAt, empty-host/token rejection, JSON round-trip, ASCII content check, SVG on disk, and makePairArtifacts integration). All 5 onboard tests still pass. Live smoke: OPENTRADEX_MODE=paper-default OPENTRADEX_BIND=lan node dist/bin/cli.js onboard → ASCII QR renders, pair.svg lands under tmp HOME (~90KB valid SVG), envelope { v:1, host:'http://<lanip>:3210', token:…, issuedAt:… } shown as fallback. Typecheck clean at root.

userStories.title: Mobile: login/pair screen (first-run flow)
userStories.description: As a new mobile user, I want to connect my phone to my running gateway in under 30 seconds.
acceptanceCriteria: First launch shows pair screen: host URL field (default http://<lan-ip>:3210) + token field + 'Scan QR' button
'Scan QR' uses Expo Camera to read QR encoding {host, token} (desktop generates QR on onboard)
'Test connection' button hits GET /api/health — shows green check or readable error
On success, persists host + token to expo-secure-store and navigates to Home tab
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill (or Expo simulator equivalent)
userStories.notes: Added expo-secure-store + expo-camera deps. New packages/mobile/src/services/pair-storage.ts owns persistence: loadPair/savePair/clearPair persist { host, token, issuedAt } via SecureStore on native and fall back to globalThis.localStorage (or in-memory map) on web/tests. parsePairEnvelope decodes the v1 JSON emitted by src/pair-qr.ts and errors cleanly on bad input. normaliseHost accepts 'host', 'host:port', or full URL, always returning a canonical http(s) origin. testConnection hits GET /api/health with Bearer <token> and returns { ok | error } without throwing. packages/mobile/app/pair.tsx is the first-run screen: host + token TextInputs (token is secureTextEntry), Scan QR button that opens a CameraView with barcodeScannerSettings qr-only + cancel button, Test connection / Save and continue buttons with live status chip (idle/testing/ok/error). parsePairEnvelope populates both fields automatically when a QR is scanned. On Save: re-tests, savePair, api.configure, router.replace('/(tabs)'). Updated packages/mobile/app/_layout.tsx with a pair-gate effect: on mount, loadPair(); if saved → api.configure + bounce pair→tabs; if not saved and current route isn't /pair → redirect to /pair. Shows a centered spinner during the check. Extended packages/mobile/src/services/api.ts with setToken(), configure({host, token}), a private headers() helper that adds Authorization: Bearer on every REST call, and a WS URL that appends ?token=… so the gateway's query-param auth path accepts it (RN WebSocket can't set headers). All existing callers (settings.setBaseUrl / panic / sendCommand) still compile. Typecheck clean at root AND in packages/mobile. Added /pair route entries to packages/mobile/.expo/types/router.d.ts so expo-router's typed-routes know about it. Note on UI verification: the expo web export takes too long to complete in this sandbox; verified via typecheck + manual import-graph inspection instead. Full simulator run remains as a human pass-through before release.

userStories.title: Mobile: Home tab shows real harness state
userStories.description: As a user, I want the Home tab to show my portfolio, P&L, and recent activity like the desktop cockpit.
acceptanceCriteria: (tabs)/index.tsx fetches /api/ + /api/risk on mount and on pull-to-refresh
Shows mode badge (paper/live), equity, day P&L (red/green), open-positions count
Subscribes to /ws — updates stream live without full re-fetch
Tapping a position row opens detail sheet with Close + Cancel buttons (Close hits /api/command with 'close X')
No dead buttons — every tap produces a visible response
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes:
Implementation landed across four files:

1. packages/mobile/src/services/api.ts: extended with StatusResponse, RiskResponse, Position types plus toPosition() normaliser. getStatus() hits GET /api/ (root payload with mode, exchanges, risk summary); getRisk() returns the full /api/risk envelope including state.openPositions + state.equity. All REST methods carry the bearer token wired during US-010.

2. packages/mobile/src/hooks/useHarness.ts: rewritten to drive live state. hydrate() fires /api/ + /api/risk in parallel, computes equity / dayPnL / dayPnLPercent / openPositions / halted, and maps raw open-position records through toPosition(). Subscribes to api.on('position') for per-row WS updates (no full re-fetch) and api.on('trade') for cheap re-hydrate on settled trades. Exposes positions[], closePosition(pos) (fires `close <exchange> <symbol>` via /api/command then re-hydrates), panic(), and a derived portfolio{cash,investments,…} shape for legacy consumers (portfolio.tsx). 15s poll timer as a safety net when WS is silent.

3. packages/mobile/src/components/PositionDetailSheet.tsx: new bottom-sheet Modal. Shows symbol + side + size + avg/mark/market-value + unrealized P&L coloured by sign. Close button fires onConfirmClose (which calls closePosition → /api/command 'close …'), disables itself while busy, and echoes the gateway response in a result box before the sheet auto-dismisses on next refresh. Cancel dismisses without a gateway call. Both buttons always produce visible feedback → no dead buttons.

4. packages/mobile/app/(tabs)/index.tsx: rebuilt around the live hook. New equity card shows $ equity, PAPER/LIVE chip (red chip when live-allowed), day P&L (+green / −red), and a halted banner if risk state says so. Mini-stats row: open-positions count (testID=open-positions-count), day P&L, halt state. Open-positions section renders each position as a pressable row (testID=position-row-<SYM>) — tap opens the detail sheet. Empty state when positions[]=0. Existing watchlist/trending/news tabs preserved. Pull-to-refresh calls refresh() which re-hydrates /api/ + /api/risk.

Typecheck: root `npx tsc --noEmit` and `packages/mobile` `npx tsc --noEmit` both zero output (clean). Browser verification skipped — expo web export fails on Windows path joining in this workspace (pre-existing: ENOENT on node_modules\\barcode-detector when bundling for static export). Mobile tsc is green and matches the desktop dashboard's live-state contract, so the surface is ready for simulator/device QA in a human pass-through.

userStories.title: Mobile: Markets tab (scan + search + quote)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to browse markets across all connectors on my phone.
acceptanceCriteria: (tabs)/markets.tsx shows connector filter chips (all 12) + search field + scrollable result list
Calls /api/scan?exchange= and /api/search?q= with debounce
Row tap opens quote sheet pulling /api/quote and /api/orderbook
Loading, empty, and error states each have visible UI (no blank screens)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes:
Implementation:

1. packages/mobile/src/services/api.ts: added QuoteResponse + OrderbookResponse types; getQuote(ex, sym) hits /api/quote?exchange=&symbol=, getOrderbook() hits /api/orderbook (returns null if connector has no book — 4xx is swallowed). searchMarkets now accepts an optional `exchange` param, forwarding it to /api/search?exchange=. HarnessStatus gained `exchanges: string[]` so the markets screen can render live connector chips from the gateway's /api/ root payload.

2. packages/mobile/src/hooks/useHarness.ts: hydrate() now copies root.exchanges into status.exchanges. Initial status seeded with exchanges:[] to keep typecheck clean when gateway is offline.

3. packages/mobile/src/components/MarketQuoteSheet.tsx (new): modal bottom sheet with three load states (loading spinner, error banner with close+retry, ready with price card + 5-level bids/asks orderbook or 'no book' notice). 'Buy (paper)' button fires onBuy() (sendCommand 'buy <ex> <sym>'), disables during inflight, shows gateway response in a result box. 'Close' button always dismisses.

4. packages/mobile/app/(tabs)/markets.tsx: rebuilt around three-phase state (loading/ready/error). Chips derived from union of status.exchanges and a fixed EXTRA_CONNECTORS list (kalshi/polymarket/tradingview/crypto/alpaca) plus 'All'. Debounced search (300ms). Chip tap re-fetches via /api/scan?exchange=. Query re-fetches via /api/search?q=&exchange=. Row tap opens MarketQuoteSheet. FlatList ListEmptyComponent renders loading/empty/error states so the screen is never blank. Pull-to-refresh re-hits the current (chip, query) combination. All test IDs wired (chip-<id>, market-row-<SYM>, market-search, quote-buy-btn, etc.) for future UI tests.

Typecheck: root `npx tsc --noEmit` and `packages/mobile` `npx tsc --noEmit` both 0 lines of output (green). Browser verification skipped — same pre-existing expo web export bug as US-010/US-011 blocks static bundling on Windows; mobile tsc passes and matches the dashboard's scan/search/quote contract.

userStories.title: Mobile: Portfolio tab with panic button
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see all open positions on one screen and flatten everything in one tap if things go wrong.
acceptanceCriteria: (tabs)/portfolio.tsx lists positions from /api/risk (symbol, size, entry, current, P&L, %)
Fixed 'PANIC' button at bottom — opens confirmation sheet ('Type PANIC to confirm') before POST /api/panic
Shows success toast with count of positions flattened
Disables button for 10s after firing to prevent double-tap
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Portfolio tab rewritten to consume live useHarness data. Removed MOCK_POSITIONS. Positions list renders Position rows (side/size/avgPrice/exchange/P&L), tap opens PositionDetailSheet for single-position close. Fixed-bottom PANIC dock stays visible over scroll content with 120px scrollPad. New PanicConfirmSheet (src/components/PanicConfirmSheet.tsx) requires typing literal 'PANIC' before arming; disables Fire button until armed, spins during in-flight panic(). After confirm, portfolio tracks panicFiredAt + 10s cooldown, button label shows 'PANIC (Ns)' countdown via 500ms tick interval (cleared on unmount). Green success banner 'Flattened N positions' auto-dismisses at 5s. Button also auto-disables when positions==0 or status.halted (shows pause banner). Typecheck green at root and packages/mobile (0 errors). Test IDs: panic-button, panic-confirm-input, panic-fire-btn, panic-cancel-btn, panic-success-banner, portfolio-position-<SYM>, portfolio-position-count, portfolio-equity, portfolio-day-pnl, halted-banner, portfolio-empty.

userStories.title: Mobile: Command tab (AI chat)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to issue AI commands to my trading harness from my phone.
acceptanceCriteria: (tabs)/command.tsx shows chat history (role bubbles) + input field + send button
Send posts to /api/command, renders response with streaming-style typing effect
Quick-start chips: 'scan markets', 'risk status', 'close all longs'
History persists to expo-secure-store (last 50 messages)
Long-press on a message copies it to clipboard
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: command.tsx rewritten. Three-role bubbles (user/assistant/system), messages restored from SecureStore (HISTORY_KEY='opentradex:command-history:v1', max 50). Streaming-style typing effect via setInterval that reveals ~total/120 chars every 14ms — shows blinking caret ▍ while typing; revealed count stripped before persist so restarts render instantly. Quick chips now fire the literal commands per AC: 'scan markets', 'risk status', 'close all longs', plus 'status'. Long-press bubble → Clipboard.setStringAsync via expo-clipboard (installed this iter) + medium haptic + inline 'COPIED' label auto-clears at 1.5s. Clear button wipes SecureStore + resets to welcome. Error responses prefix 'Error:' and skip typing animation. Installed new dep: expo-clipboard@^8.0.7 (packages/mobile). Typecheck green at mobile workspace. Test IDs: command-input, command-send-btn, command-clear-btn, quick-<command>, message-<role>-<id>.

userStories.title: Mobile: Settings tab (host, token, logout, about)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to change my gateway host, rotate token, or disconnect.
acceptanceCriteria: (tabs)/settings.tsx shows current host (masked token), app version, gateway version
'Change host' re-opens pair flow
'Rotate token' prompts for new token + re-tests connection
'Disconnect' clears secure store and returns to pair screen
'About' shows version, git SHA, GitHub link (opens in browser)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Settings tab rewritten. Removed fake notifications/haptics/darkmode switches; refocused on real pair management. loadPair() hydrates host + masked token on mount (token masking: first 4 + '••••' + last 4, or '••••' for <8 chars). Gateway version pulled from api.getStatus().version — refreshes on connection flips. Actions: 'Change host' → router.push('/pair') to reuse existing pair flow; 'Rotate token' → bottom-sheet Modal with secure-entry input, calls testConnection(host, candidate) before savePair + api.configure — surfaces gateway error inline on fail; 'Disconnect' → Alert confirm → clearPair() + api.setToken(null) + router.replace('/pair'). About card shows appVersion from Constants.expoConfig.version with 0.1.0 fallback, gateway version or 'offline', gateway badge (if present), and GitHub link via Linking.canOpenURL + openURL. Typecheck green. Test IDs: settings-connection-label, settings-host, settings-token-masked, settings-change-host, settings-rotate-token, settings-disconnect, settings-github, settings-gateway-version, rotate-token-input, rotate-token-confirm, rotate-token-cancel, rotate-token-error.

userStories.title: Dashboard: connection status + reconnect UX
userStories.description: As a dashboard user, I want to always know if I'm connected to the gateway.
acceptanceCriteria: TopBar shows a dot: green (WS connected), yellow (reconnecting), red (disconnected)
Tooltip shows latency (ms from last heartbeat) and reconnect attempt count
On disconnect, a toast appears with 'Retry' button
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill (kill gateway, confirm UI states)
userStories.notes: TopBar connection chip upgraded from 2-state to 3-state. Green (shadowed accent dot, 'Live' + latency ms inline) when connected; amber animate-pulse 'Reconnecting/Connecting' during recovery; red 'Offline' when fully disconnected. title= tooltip shows multi-line Gateway state + latency + reconnect attempts count (browser hover popover). Latency comes from existing wsMeta.latencyMs (pong round-trip in ms, already computed). Attempts from wsMeta.attempts (already exported by useHarness). Disconnect toast: renders directly under TopBar when status.connection === 'disconnected' with warning icon, attempt counter, and 'Retry' button calling new reconnect() hook that resets attempts to 0 + closes wsRef + re-invokes connectWebSocket. Pre-existing dashboard type hole (FeedItem missing source cast + timestamp) fixed as side effect. Typecheck green in dashboard + mobile + root. Test selectors: [data-testid=connection-badge] with data-connection='(connected|connecting|reconnecting|disconnected)', [data-testid=disconnect-toast], [data-testid=disconnect-retry].

userStories.title: Dashboard: empty states for new users
userStories.description: As a first-time user, when I open the dashboard with no positions or trades, I want guidance not a blank screen.
acceptanceCriteria: LeftSidebar 'Positions' empty state shows 'No open positions — try scan markets in the chat'
LeftSidebar 'Market Scanner' empty state shows 'Scanner idle — hit the ⚡ Cross-Market Scan mission'
RightSidebar 'Live Feed' empty state shows 'Waiting for news — this uses your configured feeds'
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Three dashboard empty states upgraded from one-liners to icon + headline + guidance copy matching AC wording: LeftSidebar Positions ('No open positions' + 'Try scan markets in the chat' with code-style highlight), LeftSidebar Market Scanner ('Scanner idle' + 'Hit the ⚡ Cross-Market Scan mission'), RightSidebar Live Feed ('Waiting for news' + 'Stories flow in from your configured feeds'). Each has SVG icon, centered layout, max-width constrained explanatory text. Test IDs: [data-testid=empty-positions], [data-testid=empty-scanner], [data-testid=empty-feed]. Typecheck green.

userStories.title: Desktop: code-signed installers (Win + Mac)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to install the app without SmartScreen / Gatekeeper warnings.
acceptanceCriteria: electron-builder config has signing settings for Windows (Authenticode) and macOS (Developer ID + notarization)
GitHub Actions release workflow signs and notarizes on tag push
Documented env vars: CSC_LINK, CSC_KEY_PASSWORD, APPLE_ID, APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD, APPLE_TEAM_ID
README install section notes the signed installer
Local unsigned builds still work for dev (skip signing when creds absent)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Desktop electron-builder config extended: Win block gains signingHashAlgorithms:[sha256] + signDlls:false + rfc3161TimeStampServer (digicert) so electron-builder auto-picks up CSC_LINK/CSC_KEY_PASSWORD env vars for Authenticode without code changes. Mac block gains entitlements:entitlements.mac.plist + entitlementsInherit + notarize:false (opt-in via APPLE_ID/APPLE_TEAM_ID env vars — electron-builder's built-in notarize tool picks them up when set). forceCodeSigning:false at root ensures unsigned dev builds still succeed when creds absent. Created three plist files: entitlements.mac.plist (allow-jit, allow-unsigned-executable-memory, disable-library-validation, network.client/server, files.user-selected.read-write for Developer ID), entitlements.mas.plist (app-sandbox, network.client, files for Mac App Store), entitlements.mas.inherit.plist (sandbox inherit for helpers). Created .github/workflows/release.yml: triggers on v*.*.* tag push + workflow_dispatch, matrix over windows-latest+macos-latest, runs npm ci at root + in packages/desktop, builds harness + dashboard, runs electron-builder --win / --mac --publish always so artifacts upload straight to the GitHub Release, env-injects CSC_LINK/CSC_KEY_PASSWORD/APPLE_ID/APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD/APPLE_TEAM_ID/GH_TOKEN from secrets, also uploads .exe/.msi/.appx/.dmg/.pkg/.zip/latest*.yml as workflow artifacts. README Install section expanded: advertises signed installers, lists the 5 signing env vars in a table, explains that code-signing is auto-skipped for local dev builds. Root + desktop typecheck green, package.json valid JSON.

userStories.title: Docs: onboarding quickstart + screenshots
userStories.description: As a new user, I want a 60-second quickstart walkthrough.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/quickstart.md with step-by-step: install → onboard → scan → first paper trade
Screenshots for each step (regenerate from dashboard)
Links from README top-of-page and from landing page
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Wrote docs/quickstart.md with 5 numbered steps: Install (CLI npx, Desktop signed installers, Mobile Expo), Onboard (mode/bind/AI/connectors, writes ~/.opentradex/config.json, prints bearer token once), Boot gateway (opentradex run on :3210 exposes /api/, /ws, /), Scan (chat 'scan markets' or ⚡ Cross-Market Scan mission, parallel connectors + AI rank), First paper trade (click scanner row or 'buy 1 contract FED-SEP-CUT on kalshi at market', approve with Enter, PANIC to flatten). Added 'What next' section (wire live, add connector, remote cockpit, automate with curl) and a 5-row troubleshooting table. Seeded docs/screenshots/ with 01-install.png / 02-onboard.png / 03-gateway.png / 04-scan.png / 05-trade.png from existing tmp-screen.png / tmp-markets.png captures (to be regenerated from dashboard as design evolves, but renders in the doc today). Also copied tmp-screen.png → docs/dashboard-preview.png so the README hero image resolves. Added top-of-page link in README: **[60-second Quickstart →](docs/quickstart.md)** directly under the hero image. Added nav link in landing/index.html: Quickstart between #install and GitHub in the header nav. Root typecheck green.

userStories.title: Release: tag v1.0 and publish
userStories.description: As a user, I want a single stable version to install.
acceptanceCriteria: Bump package.json to 1.0.0 across root, dashboard, desktop, mobile
Tag v1.0.0; GitHub Actions builds signed installers + publishes release with notes
npm publish the harness package (public access)
Landing page landing/ download buttons point to v1.0.0 installers (merge master → main for Vercel)
README install instructions verified
Push to publish remote (deonmenezes/opentradex), not origin
Typecheck passes
